title: TipRanks Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)

meta_description: TipRanks Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026). TipRanks (4.2★, Free). Step-by-step setup, features walkthrough, and pro tips. Updated 2026.
excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using TipRanks — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
What is TipRanks?

TipRanks is a financial accountability platform founded in 2012 by TipRanks Ltd. (headquartered in Tel Aviv, Israel) that does something most trading tools don't: it tracks and ranks Wall Street analysts, hedge fund managers, and financial bloggers based on their actual verified performance, not predictions or hype. With a 4.2/5 rating, TipRanks serves fundamental investors and research-driven traders who want to know which analysts have a track record of being right—and which have been wrong. The platform offers a free Basic tier, a $29.95/month Premium plan, and a $49.95/month Ultimate plan, making it accessible whether you're evaluating your first stock or managing a serious portfolio.

How to Create Your TipRanks Account

Getting started on TipRanks takes about 5 minutes. Here's exactly what to do:

  • Go to the TipRanks website and click "Sign Up" in the top-right corner.
  • Choose your sign-up method: You can register with an email address or connect via Google or Facebook. Email signup requires only your name, email, and a password.
  • Enter basic information: Provide your first name, last name, country of residence, and email address. TipRanks will verify your email with a confirmation link—check your inbox and click to activate.
  • No formal verification required: Unlike some brokerages, TipRanks doesn't require ID verification, employment verification, or proof of net worth to use the free or premium tiers. This makes it genuinely frictionless.
  • Choose your plan: After email confirmation, you'll be prompted to select between the free Basic tier, Premium ($29.95/month), or Ultimate ($49.95/month). You can start free and upgrade anytime—or skip this step and come back later.
  • Estimated time: Full account setup through first login: 3–5 minutes, assuming you have a working email address.

Setting Up TipRanks for the First Time

When you log in for the first time, the dashboard can feel overwhelming because there's a lot of data. Here's how to configure it for your workflow:

Dashboard Layout Overview

The main dashboard presents three zones: a top bar with search and navigation, a left sidebar with topic filters and watchlists, and a center feed showing analyst ratings, hedge fund activity, and insider trades. Don't try to absorb everything at once—most users ignore the feed and jump straight to stock research.

Configure Your Watchlist

The first thing to do is create a watchlist of stocks you're interested in. Click "Add to Watchlist" on any stock page, or use the search bar at the top to find companies. Your watchlist becomes your home base—you'll see analyst ratings, insider transactions, and Smart Scores aggregated here. This is where you spend 80% of your time on TipRanks.

Set Up Alerts

TipRanks has a built-in alerts system (available on Premium and Ultimate). Go to your account settings and configure notifications for: (1) when an analyst you follow changes a rating, (2) when insider trading activity occurs, and (3) when a stock hits a price target. On the free plan, you'll need to manually check your watchlist.

Connect to Your Broker (if using broker integration)

TipRanks integrates with major brokerages (including TD Ameritrade, E*TRADE, and others). If your broker is supported, you can optionally link your account—this lets you see your portfolio holdings directly within TipRanks. This is optional; many users skip it and just use TipRanks for research, then execute trades elsewhere.

Essential Features You Should Know

1. Analyst Rankings & Track Records

This is TipRanks' core differentiator. Every analyst on Wall Street gets a grade (A, B, C, D, or F) based on how often their Buy/Hold/Sell ratings have been correct and how many percentage points their picks have outperformed. Click on any analyst name to see their full history: number of picks, success rate, average return on successful picks, and which sectors they specialize in. If 40% of their picks are wrong, you'll see it—no hiding behind credentials.

2. Smart Score (AI Analysis)

TipRanks' proprietary AI aggregates analyst ratings, insider trading patterns, and hedge fund activity into a single 1–10 score for each stock. A score of 9–10 means very strong bullish consensus; 1–3 means bearish. Important: the Smart Score is backward-looking (based on past performance of analysts who were right), not forward-looking. It tells you what the smart money was doing, not what it will do next. Some users find this is TipRanks' best feature; others skip it entirely because it lags market moves.

3. Insider Trading Activity

Track when company insiders (executives, board members, major shareholders) buy or sell their own stock. TipRanks shows you the transaction date, number of shares, and insider name. Large insider buys by the CEO or CFO often signal confidence in the stock, while waves of insider sells can be red flags. This feature is available on all tiers and updates regularly.

4. Hedge Fund Performance Tracking

See which hedge funds are holding which stocks and how those funds have performed over time. TipRanks tracks thousands of hedge fund filings (from 13F disclosures) and lets you filter by fund size, geography, and performance. If a legendary hedge fund manager just bought a small-cap biotech stock, TipRanks will surface it. Premium and Ultimate tiers get more detailed fund analysis.

5. News Feed & Price Targets

A curated feed of analyst upgrades, downgrades, price target changes, and financial news specific to your watchlist. Instead of wading through Bloomberg or CNBC, you see only what's directly relevant to your positions. This is especially powerful if you set up alerts—you won't miss a major analyst call on one of your holdings.

6. Mobile App**

TipRanks offers iOS and Android apps that mirror the web platform. You can check analyst ratings, insider transactions, and alerts on the go. The app is clean and responsive, though it doesn't have the same depth as the desktop version. Useful for quick lookups, not for deep research.

7. Educational Content

TipRanks publishes articles, webinars, and tutorials on how to read analyst ratings, interpret insider trading, and use Smart Scores. If you're new to fundamental analysis, these resources help you understand the "why" behind the data.

TipRanks Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

Basic (Free)

$0/month. Access analyst ratings, insider trading data, and Smart Scores. You can view price targets and analyst track records. Alerts and real-time updates are limited or delayed. Best for: hobbyist investors doing occasional stock research. You can use this forever and learn a lot, but you'll miss real-time analyst calls and won't get notified of insider trades as they happen.

Premium ($29.95/month)

Add real-time alerts for analyst rating changes, insider trading, and price targets. Get access to detailed hedge fund holdings and performance analytics. Unlock more advanced filters and portfolio-level insights. Best for: serious individual traders and swing traders who want real-time notifications and don't want to miss analyst calls. Most TipRanks power users pay for Premium.

Ultimate ($49.95/month)

Everything in Premium, plus advanced AI analysis features, deeper institutional fund data, priority support, and additional research tools. Best for: professional traders, financial advisors, and money managers who use TipRanks as a core part of their workflow. If you're managing client money or trading full-time, Ultimate is worth considering.

Pricing Recommendation by Trader Type

  • Beginner (account <$10K): Start with Free. Learn how analyst ratings work, browse insider trades, and understand Smart Scores cost you nothing. Upgrade to Premium if you find yourself checking TipRanks daily.
  • Intermediate (account $10K–$100K): Premium ($29.95/month) is the sweet spot. Real-time alerts are genuinely useful for swing traders, and the $30/month cost is negligible compared to your portfolio size.
  • Advanced (account >$100K or trading full-time): Weigh Premium vs. Ultimate. If TipRanks is your primary research tool and you execute 20+ trades per month, Ultimate's extra data and analytics justify the $49.95/month. If you use it alongside other platforms (Seeking Alpha, MarketBeat), Premium is enough.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of TipRanks

1. Focus on Analyst "Success Rate" Over Star Ratings

Don't just look at how many stars an analyst has. Drill into their success rate: what percentage of their calls have been right? A 60% success rate is actually strong (market baseline is ~50%). A 70%+ success rate is exceptional. Filter by success rate first, then look at their recent calls. Many analysts have great ratings overall but are wrong on specific sectors.

2. Combine Smart Score With Insider Activity

A stock with a Smart Score of 9/10 but zero insider buying (insiders are selling) is a yellow flag. Conversely, a stock with a middling 5/10 Smart Score but the CEO just bought $5M worth of shares is worth investigating. Insiders have real money on the line; they're often smarter than the consensus.

3. Use Filters to Find Hidden Gems in Specific Sectors

TipRanks lets you filter analyst ratings by sector, market cap, and rating type. Instead of scrolling through thousands of stocks, filter for "Technology sector, under $1B market cap, with 8+ Smart Score." This narrows the haystack dramatically and surfaces stocks that are flying under the radar.

4. Track Which Analysts You Trust on Specific Sectors

Over time, you'll notice that Analyst A is great at calling biotech stocks but terrible at software, while Analyst B is the opposite. Create informal notes (or use your watchlist tags if TipRanks supports it) to track this. When you see a new call from "the biotech expert," pay attention. When the software basher calls something a sell, take it with a grain of salt.

5. Don't Use Smart Score as a Buy/Sell Signal Alone

The Smart Score is a research tool, not a trading system. It tells you where analyst consensus stands, but it's not predictive. A stock can have a 9/10 Smart Score and drop 20% in a month if macro conditions change. Use Smart Score to identify candidates worth researching deeply, not as a standalone buy signal.

6. Set Alerts on Your Top 10 Holdings**

If you own a stock, set a Premium alert for analyst rating changes on it. You don't want to miss a downgrade from a credible analyst while you're busy with other things. Alerts are the closest thing TipRanks offers to automated "heads up" notification.

7. Cross-Check TipRanks Data With a Comparison Platform

TipRanks aggregates analyst ratings, but so do Seeking Alpha and MarketBeat. If you see a major discrepancy (TipRanks says 15 Buy ratings, but Seeking Alpha shows 8), it's worth knowing why—usually it's a difference in how old or new analyst calls are included. Using two platforms as a cross-check is smarter than relying on one.

Common TipRanks Issues and How to Fix Them

Issue: Analyst Data Feels Stale or Outdated

TipRanks updates analyst ratings as firms publish them, but there can be a 1–5 day delay on the free plan. If you're on Premium and still seeing old data, log out, clear your browser cache, and log back in. If the data is truly stale (more than a week old), check TipRanks' status page or contact support—it's rare, but data feeds do occasionally lag.

Issue: Smart Score Is Too High/Low for a Stock You Know

Remember: Smart Score is backward-looking based on how accurate analysts historically have been, not on forward-looking fundamentals. If the score doesn't match your thesis, read the underlying analyst ratings and insider trades instead. Sometimes consensus is wrong, and that's when traders make money.

Issue: Broker Integration Isn't Working or Showing Old Portfolio Data

First, verify that your brokerage is supported (TD Ameritrade, E*TRADE, and others are; some smaller brokers aren't). If it's supported, disconnect and reconnect your broker account via account settings. If data is still stale after 24 hours, contact TipRanks support—the OAuth connection occasionally needs refreshing on their end.

Issue: Too Many Alerts or Missing Important Ones

Alert overload is real on Premium. Start by setting alerts only for your top 3–5 holdings, not your entire watchlist. You can always add more later. If you're missing alerts, check that notifications are enabled in your device settings (especially on mobile) and that you're logged in to the email address where TipRanks sends alerts.

Is TipRanks Worth It? Our Verdict

TipRanks earns its 4.2/5 rating because it solves a genuine problem: it shows you which analysts are actually right, not just which ones are confident. The Smart Score and insider tracking features are strong. However, most of the useful features live behind the $29.95/month Premium paywall, so the "free" part of the platform is more of a tease than a complete tool. TipRanks excels for fundamental investors and research-driven traders who want accountability in their analyst data, especially swing traders who benefit from real-time alerts. If you're a technical trader who uses TradingView or charts-first tools, TipRanks is complementary, not primary—check out Seeking Alpha for a broader research platform. TipRanks is not a replacement for a brokerage, charting tool, or screener; it's a laser-focused accountability layer on top of analyst consensus. For serious traders, the $30/month Premium tier is worth the cost.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
